decision: choose ChatGPT launch, ChatGPT Enterprise, Gemini/Bard, Claude, Copilot, Claude Code, OpenAI API tools, and Codex as the first capture wave
rationale: These eight surfaces span assistant launch, enterprise wrapper, Google response, Anthropic assistant, code-in-editor, coding agent, developer API/tools, and repository-agent packaging without requiring a screenshot per brand variant.
blocked_claims: adoption; revenue; productivity; market share; model rank; safety; autonomy; current UI
next_action: Capture and hash these first before any reserve product page.

decision: use one primary tools/GPTs/plugins screenshot, with API docs as the stronger Chapter 18 source surface
rationale: Too many OpenAI product pages would turn the visual program into a company gallery; the API/tools docs carry the platform substrate better.
blocked_claims: plugin adoption; app-store economics; tool reliability; workflow productivity
next_action: Pick plugins only for chronology; otherwise let A-0125 carry tool/API packaging.

decision: productize with prose first, reserve ChatGPT Plus for a small inset only if Chapter 6 needs a paid-product beat
rationale: Plus is historically useful but less visually distinct than launch, Enterprise, or API/tools surfaces.
blocked_claims: subscriber count; revenue; margin; retention; model quality
next_action: Hold A-0122 until final Chapter 6 layout density is known.

decision: choose Microsoft 365 Copilot only if Chapter 7 needs office-app distribution beyond ChatGPT Enterprise
rationale: Microsoft 365 Copilot is valuable as a work-surface signal, but it risks duplicating the enterprise wrapper without proving outcomes.
blocked_claims: productivity gains; seat count; ARR; customer success; security; market share
next_action: Keep as alternate to A-0123, not an automatic additional exhibit.

decision: hold Cursor as a reserve because Copilot plus Claude Code already cover code-in-editor and agent workflow
rationale: Cursor is reader-recognizable, but a third coding UI should earn its place by adding the AI-native IDE distinction.
blocked_claims: user count; productivity; code quality; market share; developer replacement
next_action: Use Cursor only if Chapter 19 needs IDE-native texture after Copilot.

decision: defer model cards, system cards, pricing pages, and benchmark pages to source-card or benchmark-specific workflows
rationale: These surfaces are evidence-rich but not product-interface screenshots; they need quote, metric, or price contracts before visual use.
blocked_claims: safety effectiveness; model rank; broad intelligence; revenue; affordability; current behavior
next_action: Route them through source-card, pricing, or benchmark passes rather than the interface screenshot queue.

decision: every interface caption must name source owner/date/surface role and explicitly avoid business-performance inference
rationale: The screenshot proves presentation and packaging, not usage, revenue, productivity, rank, security, or autonomy.
blocked_claims: adoption; revenue; productivity; market share; model rank; safety; security; autonomy; current UI
next_action: Carry blocker IDs into final captions and asset_manifest notes when screenshots are actually captured.

decision: no new assets_manifest rows in this pass because no screenshot or final artwork was created
rationale: This pass ranks and governs candidates only; manifest rows should wait for actual captured rasters, hashes, rights notes, or finished original diagrams.
